FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-26-2022 Continued To Nov-23-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jul-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-13-296939 Conservatorship of Elerwease Bradford and
PTR-19-303198 The Elerwease Bradford Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo appeared by zoom for and with Pamela Grayson Holmon by zoom.
Daniel Conrad appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Ms. Callejo addressed the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes stating that they dispute that there were misused purchases, that the Court needs to step back and look at the case with a different perspective, and spoke of the work that was done by Ms. Holmon. Ms. Callejo also stated that her Client paid over $30,000.00 of the Conservatee's expenses for many, many months until the reverse mortgage could be obtained, that her Client has never asked for fees except for the surcharge issue and that it is in the Court discretion. The Court noted that the request for fees was for $50,000.00 and then dropped to $32,000.00 when receipts had been requested. Ms. Callejo stated that if this was a Professional Fiduciary, they would be looking at an enormous amount of fees, that the surcharge was not against her Client, that it was against Ms. Witherspoon and that her Client has been doing her best. Ms. Holmon stated that the property had not had any work done to it for 30-years, that they remodeled two units to have the Seniors moved into the lower flat and then they worked on the third level, that the discrepancy was when she was paying the contractor in cash and that the contractor's bookkeeper was no longer with the contractor and she was not able to get receipts. Ms. Holmon also spoke of amazon subscriptions that got attached to purchases which got cancelled once she realized it, stated that she spends weeks at a time staying on the property to assist her Great Aunt and that she doesn't understand why the Examiner's questions are not presented all at once in their entirety. In addition, Ms. Holmon stated that she has put in her own money since early 2018. The Court stated that it is happy that Ms. Holmon came in and provided services for her Great Aunt, that it excepts the explanation that was provided for the $161.00 and that it thinks it is satisfied, and that it will be able to close out this accounting. Ms. Callejo and Mr. Conrad presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the issue of whether or not Mr. Conrad should repay the $2,356.62 in interest. Ms. Callejo also raised the issue of Mr. Conrad receiving 10% interest on his fees rather than 5%, Local Rule 14.57 being rendered irrelevant if Mr. Conrad's request is granted, the issue of other Attorneys being owed fees and not earning any interest, etc., and noted that the Conservatee's income is low and that the care are low because a family member is providing care. The Second Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and the First Report and Account of Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of First Accounting By Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-11-2021 Continued To Mar-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo appeared by zoom for Pamela Holmon.

Ms. Callejo stated that she is in communication with her Client, that her Client was hospitalized, that she communicated with her last week and that her Client was very ill. Ms. Callejo also requested a four-week continuance stating that her Client has been working with Martin Harris (Accountant) and that she was told that the Accountant needs a couple of weeks to complete the accounting. Status on filing of the first accounting is continued to March 1, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of First Accounting By Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Continued To Jan-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Franchesca Callejo for and with Pamela Grayson-Holmon.

The Court asked if there was an update on the accounting. Ms. Callejo stated that she had surgery 3 weeks ago, that her client also has some issues, that this is for a fairly short accounting period, that the only assets are from the sale and requested 4-6 weeks continuance. The Court stated it would have no problem with a continuance if the accounting can be filed by January 4, 2021. Ms. Callejo stated this is feasible. The status on filing of first accounting by trustee is continued to January 11, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
